name: Raw fish
description: Raw fish is not acutely toxic to cats, but it carries significant health risks that make it inappropriate as a regular food. Many raw fish species contain thiaminase — an enzyme that destroys thiamine (vitamin B1) — and all raw fish poses bacterial and parasitic contamination risks.
safetyNotes: Thiaminase risk: raw freshwater fish and many saltwater species (tuna, carp, herring) contain thiaminase, which destroys thiamine (B1); regular feeding causes neurological damage — weakness, seizures, head tilt, coma
Bacterial risk: Salmonella, Listeria, and other pathogens in raw fish can cause vomiting, diarrhea, and sepsis
Parasitic risk: raw fish may contain anisakis worms or other parasites that harm the GI tract
Cooked fish (plain, boneless): safe in small amounts; cooking destroys thiaminase and kills pathogens
Bones: raw fish bones can splinter and cause oral or GI injury; always remove bones before offering

symptoms.name: Based on the sources and our conversation history
Gastrointestinal Upset (Vomiting, Diarrhea, and Anorexia)
symptoms.onset: Variable. Can be acute (within hours to a few days) if caused by foodborne pathogens,
symptoms.notes: Ingesting raw fish exposes cats to foodborne pathogens like Salmonella, E. coli, and parasites, which directly cause vomiting and diarrhea12. Additionally, the high fat content in some raw fish can trigger a painful bout of acute pancreatitis1. If the cat is suffering from thiaminase-induced Vitamin

symptoms.name: Severe Neurological Dysfunction (Ataxia, Seizures, and Ventriflexion)
symptoms.onset: Chronic/Delayed (typically develops within 2 to 4 weeks of a raw fish-heavy diet).
symptoms.notes: Raw fish (such as carp, herring, and smelt) contains thiaminase, an enzyme that destroys thiamine (Vitamin B1), halting cellular energy metabolism and causing a "Wernicke-like" encephalopathy3.... Cats will exhibit severe lack of coordination (ataxia), vestibular signs, and a characteristic abnormal

symptoms.name: Ocular / Visual Impairment
symptoms.onset: 2 to 4 weeks (manifests rapidly once the thiamine deficiency encephalopathy sets in).
symptoms.notes: Alongside motor dysfunction, thiamine deficiency from raw fish consumption causes a fairly rapid onset of visual disturbances, visibly dilated pupils (mydriasis), and clinical blindness

symptoms.name: Cardiac Arrhythmias
symptoms.notes: If the thiamine deficiency caused by the raw fish is left untreated, it can severely impact the cardiovascular system. In the end stages of the disease, cats may develop a racing heart (tachycardia) and a cardiac arrhythmic syndrome with notable electrocardiographic changes

symptoms.name: Steatitis (Yellow Fat Disease)
symptoms.onset: Chronic (develops over time from an unbalanced diet).
symptoms.notes: This condition is a specific risk if a cat is fed a heavy or exclusive diet of raw or canned tuna. Tuna is extremely high in polyunsaturated fats and lacks adequate Vitamin E.
